name: The Route of the Hermitages – circular from Yebra de Basa
name_original: La Ruta de las Ermitas – circular desde Yebra de Basa
region_intro: Hiking around Yebra De Basa offers diverse landscapes within the Pyrenean foothills of Huesca, Spain. The region is characterized by the Basa Valley, featuring a mix of forests, ravines, and trails that ascend towards higher elevations. Hikers encounter natural attractions like the Cascada del Chorro and cultural landmarks such as ancient hermitages. This area provides a variety of routes, from accessible paths to more challenging treks. Best hiking trails around Yebra De Basa The most popular hiking route is The Route of the Hermitages – circular from Yebra de Basa, a 8.7 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete, connecting several traditional hermitages. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hermitage of the Knees – Saint Barbara Hermitage loop from Yebra de Basa, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.7 km) path offering views of the surrounding valley and historical sites. Local hikers also love the Hermitage of the Knees – CruzDeGualda loop from Yebra de Basa, a 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Yebra De Basa is defined by Pyrenean foothills, dense forests, and historical hermitages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more difficult ascents. The routes in Yebra De Basa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1900 hikers have used komoot to explore Yebra De Basa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Yebra de Basa?
A: Yebra de Basa offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 70 routes listed on komoot. These trails explore the diverse landscapes of the Pyrenean foothills, from valley floors to higher elevations.

Q: What types of terrain and difficulty levels can I expect on the trails?
A: The trails around Yebra de Basa cater to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy paths, moderate ascents, and more challenging treks. The region features everything from dense forests and ravines to open mountain vistas, with some routes climbing towards peaks exceeding 2500 meters in nearby areas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Yebra de Basa?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933119070/the-route-of-the-hermitages-circular-from-yebra-de-basa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Route of the Hermitages – circular from Yebra de Basa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.7 miles (13.9 km) long and connects several traditional hermitages.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Prominent natural features include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5041014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the Santa Orosia ravine. You can also find impressive viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7699247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña Oturia from Cuello Tres Cruces&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean landscape.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history, particularly known for its connection to the Hermitage of Santa Orosia. Many trails lead to or pass by ancient hermitages, such as those found on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Knees – Saint Barbara Hermitage loop from Yebra de Basa&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore uninhabited villages like Escartín, offering a glimpse into traditional architecture and the area's past.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: While many trails involve moderate ascents, there are options suitable for families looking for shorter or less strenuous walks. The area's diverse network allows for choosing paths that match different ability levels, ensuring an enjoyable experience for all.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Yebra de Basa?
A: The hiking trails in Yebra de Basa are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the historical hermitages, and the beautiful Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Can I visit abandoned villages on a hike?
A: Yes, the region offers unique opportunities to explore abandoned villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Escartín loop from Bergua&lt;/a&gt; provides a chance to discover these historical sites and their traditional architecture, adding an exploratory element to your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Yebra de Basa?
A: Yebra de Basa is generally suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. The Pyrenean foothills offer pleasant conditions in spring and autumn. Summer provides warmer weather for higher altitude treks, while winter can bring snow, requiring appropriate gear and experience for certain routes.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to significant viewpoints or summits?
A: Many trails offer rewarding views as they climb through the landscape. You can reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3255770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelupín Summit&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy expansive vistas from points such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7699247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña Oturia from Cuello Tres Cruces&lt;/a&gt;, providing memorable panoramic scenes of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful waterfalls that can be incorporated into your hikes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5041014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural attraction often included in popular hiking routes, offering a refreshing stop along the way.
tips: Route Way of the Hermitages (Yebra de Basa)

The name of this hermitage is due to the marks on the rock on which it sits, which are said to be the marks of the knees of Santa Orosia, who stops there to pray for his fate and that of his companions who flee from the Muslim leader Aben Lupo. The latter, upon reaching the place following their trail and seeing that they have passed through there, flies into a rage and leaves some marks with his sword on the rock, also visible.
Awesome. That is the word that best defines the two rock-cut hermitages and the underpass of the waterfall.
The hermitage is located inside a rock shelter, partially embedded in the stone wall.
The ascent of the Basa Valley, although without a doubt the most spectacular place of the entire route is made up of the set of cave hermitages.

The Hermitage of O Zoque is located on the edge of a large plain in the center of which sits the Sanctuary of Santa Orosia.
From this point we can see in the distance the snow-capped mountains of the Monte Perdido massif.

waypoints.name: El Chorro Waterfall and the Cave and San Cornelio Hermitages
